---
title: "Plotting Backends"
description: "tidydraws is backend-agnostic — the same tidy data powers lets-plot and plotnine"
status: new
---

`tidydraws` produces tidy Polars frames. It does not plot anything itself, and it does not depend on any plotting library. That means you can hand the same tidy frame to whichever ggplot-like backend you prefer. This page renders the **same two plots** with both [lets-plot](https://lets-plot.org/) and [plotnine](https://plotnine.org/) to make the point concrete.

## Why two backends?

| | lets-plot | plotnine |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Backend | Native SVG/HTML | matplotlib |
| Maintainer | JetBrains | Community |
| Feel | ggplot2-like, fast | ggplot2-like, familiar to matplotlib users |
| Output | Interactive HTML | Static PNG |

Both accept a pandas DataFrame. Since `tidydraws` returns Polars DataFrames, the only bridge is `.to_pandas()`.

## Setup

```{python}
import numpy as np
import arviz as az
import polars as pl
import tidydraws as td
from xarray import DataArray

np.random.seed(42)
chains, draws = 4, 500
n_groups = 4

beta = np.random.normal(1.0, 0.2, (chains, draws, n_groups))
intercept = np.random.normal(0.0, 0.5, (chains, draws, n_groups))
sigma = np.random.normal(0.8, 0.1, (chains, draws))

dt = az.from_dict(
    {"posterior": {
        "beta": DataArray(beta, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
        "intercept": DataArray(intercept, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
        "sigma": DataArray(sigma, dims=["chain", "draw"]),
    }},
    coords={"groups": [f"g{i}" for i in range(n_groups)]},
    dims={"beta": ["groups"], "intercept": ["groups"]},
)
```

## The tidy frame (backend-agnostic)

This single call produces the data both backends will plot.

```{python}
beta_df = td.spread_draws(dt, "beta[groups]")
beta_df.head()
```

## Forest plot — lets-plot

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Forest plot of `beta` by group (89% CrI) — lets-plot."

from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html()

summary = (
    beta_df.group_by("groups")
    .agg(
        pl.col("beta").quantile(0.055).alias("lower"),
        pl.col("beta").median().alias("median"),
        pl.col("beta").quantile(0.945).alias("upper"),
    )
    .sort("groups")
)

(
    ggplot(summary.to_pandas(), aes(x="groups", y="median"))
    + geom_pointrange(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper"), size=0.8)
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color="#888888")
    + labs(x="group", y="beta", title="Posterior beta by group (lets-plot)")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

## Forest plot — plotnine

The same summary frame, the same geoms, a different backend.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Forest plot of `beta` by group (89% CrI) — plotnine."

from plotnine import *

(
    ggplot(summary.to_pandas(), aes(x="groups", y="median"))
    + geom_pointrange(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper"), size=0.8)
    + geom_hline(yintercept=0, linetype="dashed", color="#888888")
    + labs(x="group", y="beta", title="Posterior beta by group (plotnine)")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

## Posterior predictive fit — lets-plot

Now a prediction-space example. We build a `DataTree` with predictions and constant data, extract with `add_epred_draws()`, and plot a ribbon + median line.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Posterior predictive fit by group — lets-plot."

n_obs = 60
x_vals = np.linspace(0, 20, n_obs)
group_labels = np.tile(np.arange(n_groups), n_obs // n_groups)

mu = np.zeros((chains, draws, n_obs))
for i in range(n_obs):
    g = group_labels[i]
    mu[:, :, i] = intercept[:, :, g] + beta[:, :, g] * x_vals[i]

pred_dt = az.from_dict(
    {
        "posterior": {
            "beta": DataArray(beta, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
            "intercept": DataArray(intercept, dims=["chain", "draw", "groups"]),
            "sigma": DataArray(sigma, dims=["chain", "draw"]),
        },
        "predictions": {
            "mu": DataArray(mu, dims=["chain", "draw", "obs_ind"]),
        },
        "constant_data": {
            "x": DataArray(x_vals, dims=["obs_ind"]),
            "group": DataArray(group_labels, dims=["obs_ind"]),
        },
    },
    coords={"groups": [f"g{i}" for i in range(n_groups)],
            "obs_ind": list(range(n_obs))},
    dims={"beta": ["groups"], "intercept": ["groups"],
          "x": ["obs_ind"], "group": ["obs_ind"]},
    pred_dims={"mu": ["obs_ind"]},
)

pred = td.add_epred_draws(pred_dt, newdata=None, var_name="mu",
                          constant_data_group="constant_data")

pred_summary = (
    pred.group_by("obs_ind", "x", "group")
    .agg(
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.055).alias("lower"),
        pl.col("mu").median().alias("median"),
        pl.col("mu").quantile(0.945).alias("upper"),
    )
    .sort("x")
)

(
    ggplot(pred_summary.to_pandas(), aes(x="x"))
    + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper", fill="factor(group)"), alpha=0.25)
    + geom_line(aes(y="median", color="factor(group)"), size=0.8)
    + labs(x="x", y="mu", color="group", fill="group",
           title="Posterior predictive fit (lets-plot)")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

## Posterior predictive fit — plotnine

Same `pred_summary` frame, plotnine rendering.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Posterior predictive fit by group — plotnine."

(
    ggplot(pred_summary.to_pandas(), aes(x="x"))
    + geom_ribbon(aes(ymin="lower", ymax="upper", fill="factor(group)"), alpha=0.25)
    + geom_line(aes(y="median", color="factor(group)"), size=0.8)
    + labs(x="x", y="mu", color="group", fill="group",
           title="Posterior predictive fit (plotnine)")
    + theme_minimal()
)
```

## Summary

- `tidydraws` is plotting-backend-agnostic because it only produces tidy data.
- The only bridge is `.to_pandas()` on the returned `DataFrame`.
- lets-plot and plotnine share near-identical `ggplot()` APIs, so the same plot is a drop-in swap.
- Choose based on output format (interactive HTML vs. static PNG) and ecosystem familiarity.
